topic: co-parenting schedule change — Amira wants to swap weekends

turns.content: quick question. amira wants to swap weekends for the holidays. like she wants this weekend and ill take the next one. is that normal
Totally normal, especially around the holidays. A lot of co-parents adjust their regular schedule to make things work with family plans and events. What's the reason she wants to swap?
she said her family is doing something on saturday and she wants ismail there. which is fine but this is my weekend technically

I get the frustration. It's your time and you planned around it. But if you'd get the following weekend instead, you're not really losing time — just shifting it. Is the next weekend one that works okay for you?
yeah next weekend actually works better for me cuz i wanted to take him to see the christmas lights on westheimer. so its not a bad trade honestly
That sounds like it works out perfectly then. He'd probably love the Christmas lights. Seems like a win-win if the next weekend actually lines up better for what you wanted to do with him.
yeah true. i just hate when plans change last minute bro. she texted me about it this morning like its no big deal

That's valid. Even when the swap works out, the short notice is annoying. It helps when both parents give more lead time on changes. Maybe worth mentioning that next time — just a heads up a few days earlier makes a difference.
wallahi i might say something. but i also dont want to start a thing over it. its not worth the drama. ill just take the swap
Yeah, picking your battles is smart. This one works in your favor anyway, so probably not the hill to fight on. Save the pushback for something that actually costs you time with him.
thats facts. plus ismail dont need to see us going back and forth about schedules. he just wants to have fun with both of us

That's a really mature way to look at it. Kids pick up on tension even when you think they don't. Keeping things smooth between you and Amira, especially around the holidays, is the best thing for him.
yeah for real. ight ima text her back and say cool. christmas lights next weekend its gonna be dope
Sounds like a plan. He's going to love it. Enjoy that time with him — those are the memories that stick.
Anytime. Have a good holiday with your little man.
